entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: C. arietinum L., the crop being studied for drought avoidance root characteristics

entities.name: ARID AND SEMI-ARID REGIONS
entities.description: Regions where chickpea productivity is constrained by terminal drought

entities.name: TERMINAL DROUGHT
entities.description: A condition that constrains the productivity of chickpea in arid and semi-arid regions

entities.name: PROLIFIC AND DEEP ROOTING
entities.description: Root characteristics that allow chickpea genotypes to be more adapted to drought

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: Environmental stress that chickpea genotypes with prolific and deep rooting are adapted to

entities.name: GENETIC CONTROL OF ROOT SYSTEM
entities.description: The study of genetic components that govern the expression of root characteristics

entities.name: ROOT AND SHOOT CHARACTERISTICS
entities.description: Physical attributes of the plant investigated through generation mean analysis

entities.name: GENERATION MEAN ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to investigate genetic components governing root and shoot characteristics

entities.name: CROSSING
entities.description: The process of creating crosses between parents with contrasting root characteristics

entities.name: ADDITIVE AND ADDITIVE X ADDITIVE INTERACTION EFFECTS
entities.description: Genetic effects that play an important role in governing root length density and root dry weight

entities.name: ROOT LENGTH DENSITY
entities.description: A root characteristic governed by additive and additive x additive interaction effects

entities.name: ROOT DRY WEIGHT
entities.description: A root characteristic governed by additive and additive x additive interaction effects

entities.name: ADDITIVE GENE EFFECTS
entities.description: Genetic effects that are consistent and towards increasing root growth

entities.name: ROOT GROWTH
entities.description: The growth of roots increased by additive gene effects

entities.name: ROOT SYSTEMS
entities.description: The overall root structure of chickpea targeted for improvement
